raw_output:
PR #135 round 1 (Claude, Fable 5): C1 MEDIUM->validated P1 jointly with Codex: after-midnight bedtime (00:30 -> 0.5) made the new callout read ~0 mg when the true modeled level is ~59 mg (numerically confirmed) — false health all-clear for after-midnight-bedtime users. C2 LOW clamp semantics negligible in real range; C3 LOW anchor flips at >18 not >=18; C4 LOW 0mg/100mg label collision >1500mg; C5 clock label edges untested; C6 tests load-bearing (interpolation pinned independently); C7 stale string in frozen acceptance doc. SECURITY: none (SVG/JSX text only, no dep/sink).
Codex round 1: P1 same after-midnight finding (model past 24:00 or account for rollover). Codex security: pure bounded interpolation, no surface.

FIX ROUND (validated P1): wrapBedtimeHour (<6 -> +24, mirrors backend _normalize_bedtime_hour), curveEndHour = max(24, wrapped+0.5), computeDecayCurve endHour param, hook passes bedtime; pinned physics: 200mg@14:00 normal -> ≈32 mg at 12:30 AM (was ≈0); formatClockLabel moved to caffeineCalc (react-refresh); clock edge tests added (C5).

RE-RUN (full battery on 32ef700, Claude): PASSES — no blocking findings. Wrap cutoff mutation-probed (<12, <5, <=6 all killed by unit tests); backend consistency byte-identical semantics; domain math verified at extremes (5:59 AM marker in-plot); hook deps correct, no curve/marker drift; no-bedtime/pre-midnight paths identical to previous head. INFO/LOW only: extended-domain visuals compress the day for after-midnight users (accepted trade); NaN bedtime would blank the chart (unreachable via Pydantic time serialization; record-only).
